description_long: This object is a traditional water well featuring a circular stone base and a cylindrical stone shaft, topped with a pitched roof made of reddish-brown wood. The roof is supported by two vertical wooden posts, and the structure includes a crossbeam and triangular wooden braces for stability. The well opening is uncovered, revealing a dark interior, and there is a visible crank handle and spindle mechanism on one side, likely for drawing water. The stonework is rendered in a block pattern, giving the well a robust and classic appearance. The overall design suggests durability and functionality, suitable for outdoor urban or park settings.
description: A classic stone water well with a pitched wooden roof, visible crank handle, and sturdy stone base. The well is open at the top and features detailed wood and stone textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pitched wooden roof, stone well opening, and partial view of the crank handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular wooden braces and the full profile of the stone base.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the roof and stone well visible but the crank handle less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the crank handle and spindle mechanism clearly, along with the stone base and wooden supports.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A crank handle and spindle mechanism are present on one side, made of wood and metal.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A low circular stone wall surrounds the well, supporting the roof and providing a barrier around the opening.
